name: Arnedo, Lodosa and Ausejo — circular through villages of La Rioja Baja
name_original: Arnedo, Lodosa y Ausejo — circular por pueblos de la Rioja Baja
region_intro: Road cycling around Calahorra offers diverse terrain, situated on a promontory overlooking the fertile plain of the Cidacos River near its confluence with the Ebro River. The region features a mix of flat plains, rolling hills, and valleys, providing varied topography for cyclists. The Cidacos Greenway, a notable feature, runs through a valley characterized by reddish karst formations, orchards, and forested areas. This landscape provides a range of road cycling routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Calahorra The most popular road cycling route is Arnedo, Lodosa and Ausejo — circular through villages of La Rioja Baja, a 47.1 miles (75.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the villages of La Rioja Baja. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Arnedillo Castle – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Calahorra, a moderate 56.8 miles (91.4 km) path. This route includes scenic views towards Arnedillo Castle and the thermal baths. Local road cyclists also love the Autol and its Picuezos – Cidacos Greenway loop from Calahorra, a 31.8 miles (51.1 km) trail leading through the Cidacos Greenway, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Calahorra is defined by fertile river plains, distinctive reddish karst formations, and varied hills and valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Calahorra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calahorra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Calahorra?
A: Calahorra offers a diverse road cycling landscape. You'll find a mix of flat plains, ideal for less demanding rides, and more varied topography with rolling hills and valleys. The region is characterized by fertile river plains, distinctive reddish karst formations, orchards, and forested areas, particularly along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4274184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: Yes, Calahorra provides options for all fitness levels. While specific 'easy' routes aren't detailed in the guide, the region offers less strenuous options, including routes up to two hours with around 1,000 feet of elevation gain, featuring mostly well-paved surfaces. There are 8 easy routes available in the area.

Q: What are some moderate road cycling routes in Calahorra?
A: For moderate rides, you could explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990755885/arnedo-lodosa-and-ausejo-circular-through-villages-of-la-rioja-baja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedo, Lodosa and Ausejo — circular through villages of La Rioja Baja&lt;/a&gt;, a 75.7 km route. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos – Cidacos Greenway loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt;, which is 51.1 km and takes you through the scenic Cidacos Greenway.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, Calahorra offers challenging routes lasting over five hours or involving more than 3,000 feet of elevation gain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31737082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lodosa – View of Sansol loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 121.4 km route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by notable attractions. You can cycle along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4274184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which showcases reddish karst formations and orchards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo Castle – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; offers views towards Arnedillo Castle. Other nearby points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Calahorra?
A: The road cycling routes around Calahorra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes fertile river plains, distinctive karst formations, and diverse hills and valleys, offering options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Calahorra are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990755885/arnedo-lodosa-and-ausejo-circular-through-villages-of-la-rioja-baja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedo, Lodosa and Ausejo — circular through villages of La Rioja Baja&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo Castle – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; are both popular circular options.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling routes in Calahorra?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly listed, the region's flat plains and less strenuous options, such as those up to two hours with moderate elevation, could be suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4274184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, known for its scenic views and generally flatter terrain, is often a good choice for family outings.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calahorra?
A: Calahorra's climate, situated in La Rioja, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to visit in Calahorra after a ride?
A: Absolutely! Calahorra is a historic city with several cultural attractions. You can explore the Gothic-style Cathedral of Santa María, wander through the medieval Jewish quarter, or visit the Municipal Museum and the Museo de la Romanización to learn about the city's Roman past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calahorra?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes available around Calahorra, catering to various skill levels. This includes 8 easy routes, 24 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.
tips: Navarrese border town located on the banks of the Ebro River, one of the most important in Spain. The area around the town is known for its famous piquillo peppers. If you pass by here, don't forget to taste them.

Ausejo is on the right bank of the Ebro. A town that is on top of the hill and which you can visit. Its population and economy is based on agriculture.

You will cycle especially among crops of asparagus, mushrooms, vines and fruit trees.

The city of Arnedo is part of Baja Rioja and is located at the foot of the Iberian System. It is a place where you can not only visit its churches, the monastery of Vico, do wine tourism or visit its cave of the hundred pillars, but also visit the area of shops and local shoe factories.
